Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no hematuria or leukocyturia. These findings suggest possible diabetic nephropathy or another chronic kidney disease, with no current evidence of urinary tract infection. Further clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.
